summary: Pass rate has recovered from 2% to 56% now that the API is returning JSON, with authority-ranking (100%) and cross-tradition (100%) performing perfectly. The biggest gaps are entity-aware queries (22%) suffering from frequent timeouts and concept-match (42%) where queries return rank-1 hits but fail text-match assertions — suggesting the right *kind* of document is found but not the specific target passage.

strengths.title: Authority weighting works as designed
strengths.detail: authority-ranking category is 100% (4/4) and authority_hit=true on essentially every failing test, confirming the post-retrieval authority multiplier in api/lib/authority.js is correctly preferring high-authority sources.

strengths.title: Cross-tradition routing is solid
strengths.detail: cross-tradition is 100% (5/5), indicating the hybrid BM25+semantic stack handles multi-corpus disambiguation well when queries are sufficiently distinctive.

strengths.title: Massive recovery from infra regression
strengths.detail: Pass rate jumped 2% → 56% after the API/HTML-response issue was resolved, validating that the prior analysis correctly diagnosed an infra problem rather than a quality regression.

critical_gaps.title: Entity-aware queries timing out
critical_gaps.detail: 5 of 9 entity-aware tests fail with 'operation aborted due to timeout' at p95=25s. These are likely triggering the full jafar-pipeline (intent → research → craft) which compounds latency, and entity resolution may be issuing too many sub-queries.
critical_gaps.evidence: entity-quddus-character-tracking, entity-letters-of-living, entity-shoghi-effendi-guardian, entity-bab-martyrdom, entity-mulla-husayn-speaker all error with timeout

critical_gaps.title: Concept-match returns rank-1 but wrong passage
critical_gaps.detail: 11 concept-match failures have rank=1 with text_hit=false. The retriever finds the correct book/author (e.g., Mahabharata for dharma, Matthew for golden rule, Sura II for zakat) but surfaces a non-target paragraph. This is a within-document ranking / paragraph-selection problem, not a retrieval problem.
critical_gaps.evidence: christian-golden-rule (Matthew, rank 1, text miss), islamic-zakat-charity (Sura II, rank 1, text miss), hindu-dharma (Mahabharata 12, rank 1, text miss), buddhist-compassion-karuna, islamic-daily-prayers

critical_gaps.title: Phrase-match misses on archaic/liturgical phrasing
critical_gaps.detail: Phrase-match queries with stylized scripture language (e.g. 'cleanse thy heart', 'Inaccessible Most High Most Holy') return null rank entirely — the target paragraph is not even in top 10. Embedding similarity likely dominates over rare-phrase BM25 here.
critical_gaps.evidence: hidden-words-cleanse-heart, long-healing-prayer-opening, aqdas-obligatory-prayer, seven-valleys-law-secret-path all rank=null

critical_gaps.title: p95 latency at 25s is unworkable
critical_gaps.detail: p50 6.4s and p95 25s indicate test timeouts (likely 25s client limit) are the direct cause of 6 errored tests. Even passing tests are slow enough to mask other regressions.

action_plan.title: Bypass jafar-pipeline for test/eval path and add per-stage timeouts
action_plan.description: The 6 timeout failures are concentrated in entity-aware tests, which likely fan out through the intent→research→craft pipeline. Add a 'raw' or 'eval' mode that runs hybridSearch directly, and wrap each pipeline stage with AbortController timeouts (e.g., 4s intent, 8s research) so a slow stage degrades gracefully rather than hanging.
action_plan.implementation: In api/lib/jafar-pipeline.js, accept an options.mode='raw' to skip intent+craft and return hybridSearch results directly. In the test runner, pass mode=raw for ranking tests. Add Promise.race with AbortController in each stage. Log per-stage latency.
action_plan.success_metric: Eliminate all 6 timeout errors; entity-aware category climbs from 22% toward 60%+; p95 drops below 10s.

action_plan.title: Boost BM25 weight and enable phrase/proximity matching for archaic queries
action_plan.description: Four phrase-match failures (cleanse thy heart, Inaccessible Most High, secret of the path, etc.) return rank=null, indicating semantic embeddings are pulling toward thematically similar but lexically wrong paragraphs. Rare-token phrases should be retrieved by BM25, not embeddings.
action_plan.implementation: In api/lib/search.js hybridSearch, increase BM25 weight in the fusion (try 0.6 BM25 / 0.4 vector vs current). Enable Meilisearch phrase matching ('"..."') when query contains ≥3 rare tokens (IDF-based heuristic). Consider Meilisearch attribute weights so paragraph text outranks title.
action_plan.success_metric: Phrase-match category 67% → 85%+; recover hidden-words-cleanse-heart, long-healing-prayer-opening, aqdas-obligatory-prayer.

action_plan.title: Improve paragraph-level reranking within correctly-retrieved documents
action_plan.description: 11 concept-match failures find rank-1 results from the *correct* book/author but the wrong paragraph. Add a second-stage reranker that scores paragraph-query overlap using token-level features (exact token hits, n-gram overlap, position of key terms) before authority weighting.
action_plan.implementation: In api/lib/authority.js (or new api/lib/rerank.js), after authority multiplication, apply a paragraph-quality boost: count of query tokens present in paragraph / paragraph length, with bonus for bigram matches. Tune on the 11 failing concept-match cases.
action_plan.success_metric: Concept-match category 42% → 65%+; specifically recover christian-golden-rule, hindu-dharma, islamic-zakat-charity.

action_plan.title: Add entity index and entity-aware query expansion
action_plan.description: Entity queries like 'Declaration of the Bab May 1844 Shiraz' or 'first Letter of the Living' need entity-grounded retrieval, not just lexical/semantic. A small entity dictionary (Bab, Mulla Husayn, Quddus, Shoghi Effendi, Letters of the Living, etc.) with associated canonical passages/aliases would dramatically help.
action_plan.implementation: Create data/entities.json with entity → {aliases, canonical_passages, related_terms}. In api/lib/search.js, detect entity mentions in query and OR-expand with aliases; boost paragraphs whose entity tags match. Eventually backfill an entity field on paragraphs.
action_plan.success_metric: Entity-aware category 22% → 60%+; recover entity-mulla-husayn-first-believer (currently rank 7), entity-declaration-bab-1844.

action_plan.title: Split timeout failure class and add stage-level metrics
action_plan.description: Continue the previous run's recommendation: distinguish client-side AbortController timeouts from server 5xx and pipeline-stage timeouts. Without this, P1 above is hard to verify.
action_plan.implementation: In the test runner, capture which stage timed out (header from API: X-Pipeline-Stage). Bucket errors as timeout-intent, timeout-retrieval, timeout-craft, http-5xx.
action_plan.success_metric: Clearer failure breakdown; faster iteration on P1.

notes_on_previous: The previous analysis correctly identified the 2% pass rate as an infra problem and explicitly warned against tuning quality knobs against noise — that restraint paid off, and the recovery to 56% confirms the underlying search stack was healthy. The recommendation to add /health smoke tests and improve error classification remains unimplemented; carrying that forward (P5) is still cheap and worthwhile. No dead ends to avoid — prior analysis made no quality-tuning changes.
